name: Structured Threat Information eXpression and Trusted Automated eXchange of Indicator Information
owner: OASIS Cyber Threat Intelligence Technical Committee — originally MITRE/DHS
description: STIX is a structured language for representing cyber threat intelligence (threat actors, campaigns, malware, indicators, observed data, courses of action, vulnerabilities). TAXII is the companion protocol for exchanging STIX data between systems (server/client pull, channels, collections). Originally developed by MITRE under DHS sponsorship; transferred to OASIS Cyber Threat Intelligence Technical Committee for standardization. STIX 2.1 is the current OASIS standard; STIX 1.x (MITRE-era XML) is legacy.
relationToCloudSecurity: STIX/TAXII underpin cloud-native threat-intelligence platforms and SIEM integrations. Most commercial threat intel feeds, ISAC sharing channels, and platform-native TI (Microsoft Defender TI, AWS Detective, Google Chronicle) speak STIX/TAXII. MITRE ATT&CK ships as STIX 2.0 bundles.
relationToAISecurity: STIX has limited AI-specific extensions today; AI-related TI is typically carried in STIX as malware/tool objects with descriptive properties. OASIS CTI TC discusses AI-specific STIX extensions but no normative AI objects yet.

license.notes: STIX/TAXII specifications are OASIS Standards under the OASIS Intellectual Property Rights Policy (Non-Assertion Mode). Implementations and the standards themselves are freely available; attribution to OASIS is expected.

lifecycle.notes: STIX 2.1 + TAXII 2.1 are the current OASIS Standards. Filed under oasis-open.org per the DNS-canonical rule — OASIS Cyber Threat Intelligence Technical Committee is the publishing organization. Previously filed under mitre.org for historical continuity. See SecID redirect at secid:reference/mitre.org/stix(/taxii) -> secid:reference/oasis-open.org/stix(-taxii).

current_extraction.notes: README-only stub. STIX/TAXII specifications are consumed directly from OASIS.

notes: Reference entry. STIX/TAXII are the dominant cyber-threat-intelligence interchange formats.
